cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why Smith became very rich. The passage says, "Smith was a landlord. He loved money very much and never gave anything to anybody. Soon he became rich." So the key part here is "never gave anything to anybody." The question is structured as a fill-in-the-blank: "Smith became very rich, because he ___ gave anything to anybody." The options are A) always, B) never, C) often, D) sometimes. From the passage, it's clear that the reason he became rich is because he didn't give anything away. The sentence directly states he "never gave anything to anybody." So the correct answer should be B) never. The other options don't
